questions.text: I'm comfortable with ambiguity in group projects and trust things will sort themselves out.
I hold the view that my comfort is a lower priority than the comfort of people I care about.
I find that I adapt to new environments without much emotional upheaval.
I focus on the content of what someone says rather than how they say it.
I feel that building a legacy requires sustained, deliberate effort over many years.
I find that separating my personal emotional reactions from my assessment of a situation allows me to act with greater clarity and purpose.
I routinely make choices that protect and advance my own interests.
I tend to cry easily — at movies, during conversations, sometimes for no clear reason.
I have a strong instinct to organize people toward a goal.
I believe that my low emotional reactivity is an authentic expression of who I am, not suppression.
I feel that my willingness to accommodate helps groups stay together.
I pick up on the subtle power plays that happen in everyday interactions.
I find that my contributions in emotionally intense situations are most valued when they come from a place of calm analysis.
I am someone who embraces and strengthens a shared mission.
I tend to miss the social significance of things like seating arrangements or eye contact patterns.
I find that self-care and personal investment are not luxuries—they are necessities.
I feel that the moral pull I experience when someone is suffering is a signal that my ethical compass is working correctly.
I resist productivity tools because they add overhead without matching how I naturally think.
